item.title: Home Parenteral Nutrition (HPN) in Adult Patients: Indications and outcome.
item.description: Clinical nutrition ESPEN - Home parenteral nutrition (HPN) is used in patients with chronic intestinal failure (IF) who cannot meet their nutritional requirements by oral and/or enteral intake and who can be safely managed o...

item.title: The plasma metabolome and clinical features of patients with coeliac disease in Northwest China.
item.description: Annals of medicine - Coeliac disease (CeD) is a relatively common autoimmune disease in Caucasian populations, but is rarely reported in China. The metabolic characteristics of Chinese patients with coeliac disease rem...

item.title: Critical Review: The Past, Present, and Future of Small Intestinal Bacterial Overgrowth (SIBO).
item.description: JGH open : an open access journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - Review - Small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO) has evolved from a severe debilitating illness to a clinical spectrum associated with abnormalities of microbial populations in the small intestine. It ...

item.title: Mesenteric adipose tissue: from protective gatekeeper to driver of inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Immunometabolism (Cobham, Surrey) - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) defines a group of diseases, including Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, characterized by chronic inflammation of the gastrointestinal tract. Evidence suggest...

item.title: Protective Effect of Liriope platyphylla Root Extract on Dextran Sulfate Sodium-Induced Ulcerative Colitis in Mice.
item.description: Journal of microbiology and biotechnology - Ulcerative colitis (UC), a major form of inflammatory bowel disease, is characterized by chronic inflammation of the colon resulting from disruption of the intestinal barrier and excessive immune a...
